conflicts.description: The prior continuity sheet treated the mother tray as Aunt Vero's owned object, but chapter 11 identifies it as communal Mothers' Drain stock bred by several ward women and merely kept by Vero.
conflicts.canonical_value: The Sump Ward mother tray is communal Mothers' Drain stock kept by Aunt Vero for the communal houses, not privately owned by her.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 11 explicitly says the strain was made by several women and 'not enough to own one,' so the updated sheet uses keeper language rather than sole ownership.

conflicts.description: The prior continuity sheet treated the first chorus board as Silas-owned, but chapters 15-17 show the built boards as coalition-made public infrastructure and later board work as shared labor with no sole credit.
conflicts.canonical_value: Bellglass chorus boards are coalition-built, revisable public boards, and the later Ferrymouth kitchen board is tracked as shared labor rather than sole Silas ownership.
conflicts.resolution_note: Silas conceives the format, but the manuscript shows the boards being assembled, corrected, duplicated, and governed by an explicit no-sole-credit rule.
